content_body:
Plan your winter cabin trip to Blue Ridge, GA and experience the beauty and serenity of the mountains. Winter in Blue Ridge is a beautiful and peaceful time of year, offering many things to do both inside and outside.

The town is much quieter than summer and fall, and it’s the perfect time for a relaxing getaway in a luxury cabin rental, away from the hustle and bustle of the city. Weather-wise, Blue Ridge winters stay very mild for the most part, with highs in the low 40s to upper 50s.


Cohutta Sunset ~ 3b/3b ~ Sleeps 6

Reserve one of our cabins with hot tubs and experience a romantic getaway to North Georgia. Perfect for one couple or five, our romantic cabin rentals in Blue Ridge are the answer to rekindling your relationship. This is what the mountains were made for!  

Spa Day in Blue Ridge

Need we say more? Treat yourself to a massage in the Blue Ridge Mountains at Serenity in the Mountains or Tea Tree's Boutique Spa. If staying in is what your heart desires, make sure to check out Tea Tree's in-cabin massage service to bring the spa day to you!

Best Winter Activities In Downtown Blue Ridge 

Downtown Blue Ridge offers some of the most unique shops and boutiques on Main Street so that you could easily spend an entire day shopping! Paradise Beauty Company is our favorite women's boutique, and for the guys in your group, OAR Finer Goods offers leather goods, men's fragrances, vintage memorabilia, and more.

For the kids young and old, check out 2nd Story Entertainment - an 18-hole miniature golf course running through different rooms in a 100-year-old hotel! Vertigo Pinball and Escape Blue Ridge offer other kid-friendly activities in downtown Blue Ridge that will keep your family entertained for ages.

Blue Ridge is known for its variety of dining options and boasts almost every type of cuisine your heart desires. One of our favorites is Ferraro's Kitchen — a lovely spot to savor incredible Italian food and cocktails. Mystic Mountain Pizza is another fan-favorite, perfect for bringing the deliciousness back to your cabin! For a great after-dinner spot to satisfy that sweet tooth, visit Mona Lisa's Dessert House for homemade sweet treats and a luxe craft cocktail. 

Enjoy a glass of wine at any of Blue Ridge's cozy wine-tasting rooms or a cold glass of beer at a couple of our craft breweries in town. For wine enthusiasts, The Vine is a unique wine-tasting establishment combined with a small-town feel, enveloping you in the warmth of Blue Ridge's friendliness. Grumpy Old Men and Tipping Point Brewing Co. are just a couple of fun breweries to try in Downtown. Experience small-town camaraderie in a cozy atmosphere and feel a part of the heartbeat that is Blue Ridge.

Ice Skating in Downtown Blue Ridge

No winter vacation would be complete without ice skating! Located at the Blue Ridge Farmers Market next door to The Swan Drive-In, Blue Ridge on Ice is the perfect winter activity from late November to early January.

Blue Ridge, GA Winter Hikes

The North Georgia Mountains provide some of the most breathtakingly beautiful hikes on this side of the Mississippi. Winter hikes are enjoyable for several reasons: long-range mountain views, no pesky bugs, and crisp air!

A must for your winter bucket list is visiting the tallest mountain in Georgia — Brasstown Bald. Experience stunning 360 degree views and view 4 states from your vantage point! If you want to experience a bit of the Appalachian Trail, Springer Mountain, the beginning of this famed path, is nearby and is located nearby Amicalola Falls, the third tallest waterfall east of the Mississippi.

Guided Fly-fishing Trips on the Toccoa River and Private Streams

Blue Ridge, GA, is the Trout Capital of Georgia, and for good reason! Our rivers and streams are home to blue ribbon rainbows and browns, and our fishing guides are enthusiastic about helping you land one of these beauties.

We have partnered with North GA Trout Fishing where Joe DiPietro will take you and your crew on a guided fly-fishing trip for all skill levels. They can take you out on the Toccoa River or on our private ½ mile stretch of famed trout fishing on Noontootla Creek and create a memory for you that you will not soon forget. During these cool winter months, fishing is at its prime, and you are assured of the catch of a lifetime!

Winter Train Ride on the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway

On weekends in December in Downtown Blue Ridge, you’ll find the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway Christmas Express. During this 1-hour Santa Express ride, your family will hear a Christmas story, enjoy Christmas songs, and, of course, visit with Santa and Mrs. Claus. There will be plenty of chances to snap the perfect picture, so don't forget your camera. 

During the winter months, the train runs between Blue Ridge, GA and McCaysville, GA/Copperhill, TN. Stay warm in one of their heated, enclosed cars and enjoy the passing beauty of the Toccoa River! 

Live Performances at the Blue Ridge Community Theater

Blue Ridge has its own community theater producing some of the region's best plays and live music performances. This Blue Ridge highlight is a staple of the community, and they never fail to impress with their monthly productions.

Annual Blue Ridge Fire and Ice Festival

Visit Downtown Blue Ridge for the annual February festival called the Fire and Ice Festival. Taste test delicious Chili, vote on your favorite, enjoy ice sculptures, and enjoy a craft beer or local wine from the festival's vendors.

Fields of the Wood Bible Park

Fields of the Wood Bible Park, located in nearby Murphy, NC, is claimed to be the #1 Family Attraction in the Entire Smokies Area. Experience the peacefulness that Fields of Woods Bible Park offers; free to all who enter, from sunrise to sunset. Here you will find the Word of God beautifully engraved in stones all throughout the many acres of this serene park.

Winter in Blue Ridge offers no shortage of things to do for families, couples, and friends to explore. Start planning your winter getaway and browse our luxury cabins here. (706) 432-2140
title: Winter Things To Do in Blue Ridge, GA
